29: Given a file name and line number of a macro definition, return its related operations
30: 
31: ```cypher
32: MATCH (op:OPERATIONS)
33: WHERE op.location[0] = 'M'
34:   AND op.location[7] ENDS WITH $file_name
35:   AND toInteger(op.location[8]) = $line
36: RETURN op.uid, op.name, op.location
37: ORDER BY op.uid
38: LIMIT 50;
39: ```
40: 
41: ## One-step Enclosing
42: 
43: Given an operation uid, return its enclosing operation, such as `builtin.module`, `hl.func`, `core.scope`, `hl.if`
44: 
45: ```cypher
46: MATCH (:OPERATIONS {uid: $op_uid})<-[ie:CHILDINST]-()<-[:CHILDBLOCK]-()<-[re:CHILDREGION]-(enclos:OPERATIONS)
47: RETURN enclos.uid, enclos.name, enclos.location, ie.idx, re.idx;
48: ```
49: 
50: ## Enclosing Function
51: 
52: Given an operation uid, return its enclosing function
53: 
54: ```cypher
55: MATCH (:OPERATIONS {uid: $op_uid})
56:   (()<-[:CHILDINST]-()<-[:CHILDBLOCK]-()<-[:CHILDREGION]-()){1,}
57:   (func:OPERATIONS {name: "hl.func"})
58: RETURN func.uid, func.func_name, func.location;
59: ```
60: 
61: ## One-step Child Operations
62: 
63: Given an operation uid, return operations directly contained in its immediate child regions and blocks.
64: 
65: ```cypher
66: MATCH (:OPERATIONS {uid: $op_uid})-[re:CHILDREGION]->()-[:CHILDBLOCK]->()-[ri:CHILDINST]->(child:OPERATIONS)
67: ORDER BY re.idx, ri.idx
68: RETURN child.uid, child.name, child.location, re.idx, ri.idx
69: LIMIT 100;
70: ```
71: 
72: ## Child Operations
73: 
74: Given an operation uid, return operations contained under it at any nesting depth.
75: 
76: - Add a `WHERE` filter when only certain operation kinds are needed, such as `hl.call`.
77: 
78: ```cypher
79: MATCH (:OPERATIONS {uid: $op_uid})
80:   (()-[:CHILDREGION]->()-[:CHILDBLOCK]->()-[:CHILDINST]->()){1,}
81:   (child:OPERATIONS)
82: RETURN child.uid, child.name, child.location
83: LIMIT 200;
84: ```
85: 
86: ```cypher
87: MATCH (:OPERATIONS {uid: $op_uid})
88:   (()-[:CHILDREGION]->()-[:CHILDBLOCK]->()-[:CHILDINST]->()){1,}
89:   (child:OPERATIONS)
90: WHERE child.name IN ["hl.call"]
91: RETURN child.uid, child.name, child.location
92: LIMIT 200;
93: ```
94: 
95: ## Name to Function
96: 
97: Given a function name, return a matching `hl.func` operation.
98: 
99: - Use `func_name` when the name comes from VAST DB query results. It is the demangled function name with qualifiers and prototype, such as `NS::C::foo(int *)`.
100: - Use `fs_raw_name` when the name comes from source code. It is the source-level function name, such as `foo`.
101: - Prefer a function definition; if no definition exists, return a declaration. `is_def` indicates whether the returned `hl.func` has a body.
102: 
103: ```cypher
104: MATCH (func:OPERATIONS {name: "hl.func", func_name: $func_name})
105: OPTIONAL MATCH (func)-[e:CHILDREGION]->()-[:CHILDBLOCK]->()
106: WITH func, e IS NOT NULL AS is_def
107: ORDER BY is_def DESC LIMIT 1
108: RETURN func.uid, func.func_name, func.location, is_def;
109: ```
110: 
111: ```cypher
112: MATCH (func:OPERATIONS {name: "hl.func", fs_raw_name: $func_raw_name})
113: OPTIONAL MATCH (func)-[e:CHILDREGION]->()-[:CHILDBLOCK]->()
114: WITH func, e IS NOT NULL AS is_def
115: ORDER BY is_def DESC
116: WITH func.func_name AS func_name, collect(func)[0] AS func, collect(is_def)[0] AS is_def
117: RETURN func.uid, func.func_name, func.location, is_def;
118: ```
119: 
120: ## Name to Variable
121: 
122: Given a variable name, return matching `hl.var` operations.
123: 
124: ```cypher
125: MATCH (var:OPERATIONS {name: "hl.var"})
126: WHERE var.varName ENDS WITH $var_name
127: RETURN var.uid, var.varName, var.location;
128: ```
129: 
130: ## Name to Struct/Union/Class
131: 
132: Given a struct/union/class name, return matching `hl.struct`/`hl.union`/`hl.class` operations.
133: 
134: - For struct in C, op name is `hl.struct`
135: - For struct and class in C++, op name is `hl.class`
136: - For union, op name is `hl.union`
137: 
138: ```cypher
139: MATCH (s:OPERATIONS {name: "hl.struct"})
140: WHERE s.structName ENDS WITH $struct_name
141: RETURN s.uid, s.structName, s.location;
142: ```
143: 
144: ```cypher
145: MATCH (c:OPERATIONS {name: "hl.class"})
146: WHERE c.className ENDS WITH $class_name
147: RETURN c.uid, c.className, c.location;
148: ```
149: 
150: ```cypher
151: MATCH (u:OPERATIONS {name: "hl.union"})
152: WHERE u.unionName ENDS WITH $union_name
153: RETURN u.uid, u.unionName, u.location;
154: ```
155: 
156: ## Name to Typedef
157: 
158: Given a typedef/using name, return matching `hl.typedef` operations
159: 
160: ```cypher
161: MATCH (td:OPERATIONS {name: "hl.typedef"})-[:ARGATTR {idx: 0}]->(s:STRINGATTRS)
162: WHERE s.value ENDS WITH $typedef_name
163: RETURN td.uid, s.value, td.location;
164: ```
165: 
166: # Call Chain
167: 
168: ## Direct Caller
169: 
170: Given a callee `hl.func` uid, return caller `hl.func` operations and `hl.call` operations.
171: 
172: ```cypher
173: MATCH (callee:OPERATIONS {uid: $callee_uid, name: "hl.func"})
174: MATCH (:STRINGATTRS {hash: callee.hash})<-[:ARGATTR]-(call:OPERATIONS {name: "hl.call"})
175:   (()<-[:CHILDINST]-()<-[:CHILDBLOCK]-()<-[:CHILDREGION]-()){1,}
176:   (caller:OPERATIONS {name: "hl.func"})
177: ORDER BY caller.uid
178: RETURN caller.uid, caller.func_name, caller.location, call.uid, call.location;
179: ```
180: 
181: ## Call Operation to Callee
182: 
183: Given an `hl.call` uid, return its callee `hl.func` operation.
184: 
185: - Prefer a function definition; if no definition exists, return a declaration. `is_def` indicates whether the returned callee has a body.
186: 
187: ```cypher
188: MATCH (call:OPERATIONS {uid: $call_uid, name: "hl.call"})-[:ARGATTR {idx: 0}]->(s:STRINGATTRS)
189: MATCH (callee:OPERATIONS {name: "hl.func", hash: s.hash})
190: OPTIONAL MATCH (callee)-[e:CHILDREGION]->()-[:CHILDBLOCK]->()
191: WITH callee, e IS NOT NULL AS is_def
192: ORDER BY is_def DESC LIMIT 1
193: RETURN callee.uid, callee.func_name, callee.location, is_def;
194: ```
195: 
196: ## Direct Callees
197: 
198: Given a caller `hl.func` uid, return each direct `hl.call` operation inside it and the resolved callee `hl.func` operation.
199: 
200: - This combines `Child Operations` with `Call Operation to Callee`.
201: - For each call, prefer a callee function definition; if no definition exists, return a declaration. `is_def` indicates whether the returned callee has a body.
202: 
203: ```cypher
204: MATCH (:OPERATIONS {uid: $caller_uid, name: "hl.func"})
205:   (()-[:CHILDREGION]->()-[:CHILDBLOCK]->()-[:CHILDINST]->()){1,}
206:   (call:OPERATIONS)
207: WHERE call.name IN ["hl.call"]
208: CALL {
209:   WITH call
210:   MATCH (call)-[:ARGATTR {idx: 0}]->(s:STRINGATTRS)
211:   MATCH (callee:OPERATIONS {name: "hl.func", hash: s.hash})
212:   OPTIONAL MATCH (callee)-[e:CHILDREGION]->()-[:CHILDBLOCK]->()
213:   WITH callee, e IS NOT NULL AS is_def
214:   ORDER BY is_def DESC LIMIT 1
215:   RETURN callee, is_def
216: }
217: RETURN callee.uid, callee.func_name, callee.location, is_def, call.uid, call.location;
218: ```
219: 
220: # Data Flow
221: 
222: ## One-step Use to Def
223: 
224: Given an operation uid and an operand index, return the operation that directly defines that operand value.
225: 
226: - This follows one `ARGVALUE -> VALUES <- RESVALUE` step.
227: 
228: ```cypher
229: MATCH (:OPERATIONS {uid: $op_uid})-[:ARGVALUE {idx: $arg_idx}]->(v:VALUES)<-[:RESVALUE]-(def:OPERATIONS)
230: RETURN def.uid, def.name, def.location;
231: ```
232: 
233: ## Use to Def
234: 
235: Given an operation uid and an operand index, walk backward through def-use links and return the source definition operation.
236: 
237: - The final definition is limited to `hl.var`, `hl.param`, `hl.call`, `hl.indirect_call` or `hl.globref`.
238: - Intermediate operations are returned as `mid_infos`.
239: - Paths with an intermediate `hl.call` or `hl.indirect_call` are excluded.
240: 
241: ```cypher
242: MATCH (:OPERATIONS {uid: $op_uid})-[:ARGVALUE {idx: $arg_idx}]->()
243:   (()<-[:RESVALUE]-(mids)-[mid_args:ARGVALUE]->()){0,}
244:   ()<-[:RESVALUE]-(def:OPERATIONS)
245: WHERE def.name IN ["hl.var", "hl.param", "hl.call", "hl.indirect_call", "hl.globref"]
246:   AND NONE(mid IN mids WHERE mid.name IN ["hl.call", "hl.indirect_call"])
247: RETURN
248:   def.uid, def.name, def.location,
249:   [
250:     i IN range(0, size(mids) - 1) |
251:     {
252:       uid: mids[i].uid,
253:       name: mids[i].name,
254:       location: mids[i].location,
255:       argidx: mid_args[i].idx
256:     }
257:   ] AS mid_infos;
258: ```
259: 
260: ## One-step Def to Use
261: 
262: Given an operation uid, return operations that directly use its result value.
263: 
264: - Most operations have one result value, so this template does not filter `RESVALUE` by edge `idx`.
265: - This follows one `RESVALUE -> VALUES <- ARGVALUE` step.
266: 
267: ```cypher
268: MATCH (:OPERATIONS {uid: $op_uid})-[:RESVALUE]->(v:VALUES)<-[ae:ARGVALUE]-(use:OPERATIONS)
269: RETURN use.uid, use.name, use.location, ae.idx;
270: ```
271: 
272: ## Global Symbol References
273: 
274: Given a global variable or function name, return `hl.globalref` operations that produce symbol-reference values.
275: 
276: - Operations that use a global variable or function consume the result value of `hl.globalref`, not a result value from the global `hl.var` or `hl.func`.
277: - For function symbols, this means function-pointer uses, not direct `hl.call` calls.
278: - For function symbols, `$sym_name` is the `func_name`, not the `fs_raw_name`.
279: 
280: ```cypher
281: MATCH (ref:OPERATIONS {name: "hl.globalref", symName: $sym_name})
282: RETURN ref.uid, ref.location;
283: ```
284: 
285: ## Def to Call Argument
286: 
287: Given an operation uid, follow its result value forward and return call operations that receive it as an argument.
288: 
289: - Intermediate operations are returned as `mid_infos`.
290: - Paths with an intermediate `hl.call` or `hl.indirect_call` are excluded.
291: 
292: ```cypher
293: MATCH (:OPERATIONS {uid: $op_uid})-[:RESVALUE]->(:VALUES)
294:   (()<-[mid_args:ARGVALUE]-(mids)-[:RESVALUE]->()){0,}
295:   ()<-[arg:ARGVALUE]-(call:OPERATIONS)
296: WHERE call.name IN ["hl.call", "hl.indirect_call"]
297:   AND NONE(mid IN mids WHERE mid.name IN ["hl.call", "hl.indirect_call"])
298: RETURN
299:   call.uid, call.name, call.location, arg.idx,
300:   [
301:     i IN range(0, size(mids) - 1) |
302:     {
303:       uid: mids[i].uid,
304:       name: mids[i].name,
305:       location: mids[i].location,
306:       argidx: mid_args[i].idx
307:     }
308:   ] AS mid_infos;
309: ```
310: 
311: ## Def to Assign
312: 
313: Given an operation uid, follow its result value forward and return `hl.assign` operations where it becomes the LHS destination.
314: 
315: - The returned `value_op` defines the RHS source value assigned into that destination.
316: - Intermediate operations are returned as `mid_infos`.
317: - Paths with an intermediate `hl.call` or `hl.indirect_call` are excluded.
318: 
319: ```cypher
320: MATCH (:OPERATIONS {uid: $op_uid})-[:RESVALUE]->(:VALUES)
321:   (()<-[mid_args:ARGVALUE]-(mids)-[:RESVALUE]->()){0,}
322:   ()<-[:ARGVALUE {idx: 1}]-(assign_op:OPERATIONS)-[:ARGVALUE {idx: 0}]->(:VALUES)<-[:RESVALUE]-(value_op:OPERATIONS)
323: WHERE assign_op.name IN ["hl.assign"]
324:   AND NONE(mid IN mids WHERE mid.name IN ["hl.call", "hl.indirect_call"])
325: RETURN
326:   assign_op.uid, assign_op.name, assign_op.location,
327:   value_op.uid, value_op.name, value_op.location,
328:   [
329:     i IN range(0, size(mids) - 1) |
330:     {
331:       uid: mids[i].uid,
332:       name: mids[i].name,
333:       location: mids[i].location,
334:       argidx: mid_args[i].idx
335:     }
336:   ] AS mid_infos;
337: ```

(End of file - total 337 lines)
</content>

parts.text:
Looking at the source code again:
- Line 532 calls gaussian_blur with `width, height` and `tmpbuf, width, in->data[p], in->linesize[p]`
- The gaussian_blur function starts at line 454

In gaussian_blur:
- It receives `w` and `h` as parameters
- At lines 460-461 and 491-492, it copies `w` bytes using memcpy
- The issue is that it receives `dst_linesize = width` and `src_linesize = in->linesize[p]` 

The key insight from the code at line 532-534:
```c
gaussian_blur(ctx, width, height,
              tmpbuf,      width,        // dst_linesize = width
              in->data[p], in->linesize[p]); // src_linesize = actual linesize
```

The vulnerability is:
- The function copies `w` (which is `width` from the plane) bytes in each memcpy call
- But the actual source linesize (`in->linesize[p]`) may be different from `width`
- If `in->linesize[p]` < `width`, then the memcpy reads out of bounds

The source linesize can be smaller than the nominal width in certain cases (e.g., due to memory alignment, or when the image dimensions don't match the allocated buffer).
